summary.mini_title: Platformer: Substack's Promotion of Extremism as Policy Outc
summary.brief_summary: Casey Newton, likely via Platformer, critiques Substack's promotion of a pro-Nazi newsletter. He argues this was an inevitable outcome of the platform's policies, not an error. The incident involved a push notification for 'NatSocToday,' a white nationalist publication.

detailed_summary.issue_thesis: Substack's push notification promoting a pro-Nazi newsletter was an 'inevitable' outcome of its platform policies, stemming from the conflict between its self-perception as 'neutral infrastructure' and its use of algorithmic promotion tools.

sections.heading: Incident Description
bullets: Substack sent a push alert for 'NatSocToday,' a newsletter for the 'National Socialist and White Nationalist Community,' to an undisclosed number of users.
The newsletter's content, as reported by Taylor Lorenz, describes Jewish people as a 'sickness' and calls for the eradication of minorities.
Substack's recommendation system also suggested other white nationalist publications to users who clicked on the newsletter's profile.

sections.heading: Substack's Response
bullets: Substack framed the event as a 'serious error' and apologized for the 'extremely offensive' notifications.
The platform took the recommendation system offline for diagnosis following the incident.

sections.heading: Casey Newton's Critique
bullets: Casey Newton argues the event was a predictable consequence of Substack's decision to permit and monetize pro-Nazi publications.
He contends that the platform's use of 'social media-style growth hacks' would eventually lead to the active promotion of extremist content.
Newton's publication, Platformer, had previously left Substack over this very issue.

sections.heading: Implications and Vindication
bullets: The event serves as a vindication for Newton, whose earlier departure was framed by critics (e.g., Glenn Greenwald) as a 'pro-censorship crusade' or a subscription stunt.

conclusions_or_recommendations: A platform cannot remain neutral infrastructure while simultaneously employing growth-oriented algorithms that can amplify any content it monetizes, including extremist material.

summary.mini_title: Organic Chemistry Research Digest: Catalysis, Synthesis, & F
summary.brief_summary: This digest presents recent advancements across organic chemistry, targeting researchers and practitioners. It covers new catalytic methods for C-C and C-X bond formation, enantioselective reactions, and complex molecule synthesis. Key sections detail method development, total synthesis of natural products, and functionalization techniques relevant to medicinal chemistry.

detailed_summary.publication_identity: Not specified; appears to be a digest of recent academic research.
detailed_summary.issue_thesis: A compilation of recent breakthroughs in organic chemistry, focusing on new catalytic methods, total synthesis of complex natural products, and functionalization techniques relevant to medicinal chemistry.

sections.heading: Catalysis and Method Development
bullets: A nickel-catalyzed, single-vessel, open-to-air method enables deoxygenative cross-coupling of two different alcohol fragments to form C(sp³)–C(sp³) bonds, proposed via an S H 2 radical sorting process (MacMillan, *Science*).

A copper-catalyzed enantioconvergent N-alkylation couples diverse (hetero)aromatic amines with secondary/tertiary alkyl halides, yielding over 110 chiral amine examples via an outer-sphere nucleophilic attack (Liu/Yu/Li, *JACS*).

A cooperative photoredox/Fe/chiral primary amine triple catalysis system was developed for the enantioselective construction of all-carbon quaternary stereocenters from unactivated alkyl sources (Yang, *JACS*).

An organic helicenium photoredox catalyst facilitates wavelength-dependent C–X activation: red light for C(sp²)–I via halogen atom transfer (XAT), and blue light for C(sp²)–Br via consecutive photoinduced electron transfer (conPET) (Gianetti, *JACS*).

A NiH-catalyzed regio- and enantioselective hydrofluorination of unactivated alkenes forms C–F bonds using a coordination directing strategy and a tailored chiral Bn-BOx ligand, showing a (+)-nonlinear effect (Hong, *JACS*).

A nickel-catalyzed platform performs kinetically controlled, stereodivergent one-carbon transposition of terminal alkenes to either *E*- or *Z*-internal alkenes at room temperature (Engle/Vantourout, *Angew. Chem.*).

A Cu(I)-catalyzed asymmetric propargylic substitution of ketone-derived propargylic carbonates with N-, O-, and C-centered nucleophiles produces chiral α-tertiary ethynylamines and related structures (Zhou/Wang, *Nat. Chem.*).

A bifunctional chiral phosphoric acid containing a polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) photosensitizer catalyzes highly enantioselective [2π + 2σ] photocycloaddition of bicyclo[1.1.0]butanes with vinylazaarenes, forming bicyclo[2.1.1]hexane derivatives (Jiang, *JACS*).

Substituted seven-membered oxacycles (oxepanes) are synthesized via Rh(I)-catalyzed skeletal remodeling of 4-hydroxy-2-cyclobutenones, involving C–C bond formation and subsequent C(sp³)–C(sp³) bond cleavage (Sarpong/Baik, *J. Org. Chem.*).

sections.heading: Total Synthesis
bullets: An enantioselective, divergent synthesis produced 10 diterpenoid pyrones, including the first enantioselective synthesis of higginsianins A, B, D, E, and metarhizin C, from chiral pool enoxolone (Li, *JACS*).

The first total synthesis of pallamolides A–E was achieved, introducing asymmetry via Corey-Bakshi-Shibata kinetic resolution and featuring diastereoselective Michael additions and SmI₂-mediated cyclization (Jia, *Angew. Chem.*).

The first total synthesis of the Euphorbia diterpenoid pepluacetal was completed in both racemic and chiral forms, featuring a photo-induced Wolff rearrangement/lactonization cascade and a late-stage Rh-catalyzed transannular C(sp³)–H carbenoid insertion (She, *Angew. Chem.*).

sections.heading: Functionalization and Medicinal Chemistry
bullets: A photocatalytic hydroarylation enables late-stage functionalization of dehydroalanine (Dha) residues in peptides using arylthianthrenium salts, compatible with sensitive functional groups (Noel, *Angew. Chem.*).

A (3+2) cycloaddition of *in situ*-generated difluoromethyl nitrile oxide with alkenes forms CF₂H-isoxazolines, convertible into medicinally relevant building blocks and used for late-stage modification of drugs (Mykhailiuk, *Org. Lett.*).

A metallaphotoredox cross-electrophile coupling between aryl bromides and benzyl bromides to form di(hetero)arylmethanes was rapidly optimized using high-throughput experimentation (HTE) and ChemBeads (Rauch, *Org. Lett.*).

summary.mini_title: beehiiv: Email Boosts Create In-Platform Ad Network
summary.brief_summary: beehiiv announced 'Email Boosts,' an expansion of its cross-promotion network. This feature enables newsletter creators to embed paid recommendations for other beehiiv newsletters directly into their email content, reaching their entire subscriber list. Positioned as a self-contained, two-sided marketplace, it leverages first-party data for subscriber quality verification.

detailed_summary.issue_thesis: beehiiv launched 'Email Boosts,' expanding its 'Boosts' cross-promotion network to allow newsletter creators to embed paid recommendations for other beehiiv newsletters directly into their email content, creating an in-platform ad network for growth and monetization within the beehiiv ecosystem.

sections.heading: Evidence & Performance
bullets: The pre-launch 'Web Boosts' network reportedly paid 'hundreds of thousands of dollars' to publishers and generated 'nearly half a million' new subscribers for advertisers ('Growers').
Rowan Cheung of The Rundown AI acquired over 10,000 subscribers via the Boosts network at a cost per acquisition (CPA) of $2.00.
Marketplace offers in the network go up to $4.50 per verified subscriber.
Recommendations utilize 'Magic Link' technology for 1-click, auto-subscribe actions.

sections.heading: Mechanics
bullets: The entire system is designed for use without code, custom integrations, or APIs.

**For Growers (Advertisers):** Create an offer with a set cost per new subscriber, enable for 'Email Boosts,' define a 'max spend' cap per publisher/email, approve publisher applications and individual send requests. Upon approval, an amount equal to 'max spend' is held in escrow for 72 hours, with Growers only charged for verified subscribers and unused funds returned.

**For Publishers (Monetizers):** Browse the marketplace for email-enabled offers, apply for Grower approval, and once approved, submit a new request for each individual newsletter send. If the per-send request is accepted, the publisher has a 72-hour window to send the email with the Boost added via the beehiiv editor.

sections.heading: Implications & Constraints
bullets: The Boosts network is exclusive to users on beehiiv's 'Scale' or 'Enterprise' plans, creating an incentive for platform lock-in.
The system enables a 'reinvestment flywheel,' allowing creators to use revenue from publishing Boosts to fund their own growth campaigns.
The per-send approval process gives Growers granular control over partnerships and budget, but adds an administrative layer, with 72-hour escrow and send windows enforcing a structured workflow.
beehiiv's first-party data and engagement metrics are presented as ensuring higher-quality subscribers compared to other paid acquisition channels.
Email Boosts create a self-contained ad network that directly competes with external newsletter cross-promotion tools and general ad platforms.
